title: LDAP
text: LDAP The Lghtweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) is a protocol for accessing online directory services. It offers common access to a centralized facility for storing and maintaining user access privilege, email, and sign-on information which can be shared among Web servers, mail servers, news servers and Uniface applications. LDAP runs directly over TCP/IP or TLS, and can be used to access a stand-alone LDAP directory service or to access a directory service that uses an X.500 standards-compliant database. A directory is similar to a database, but tends to contain more descriptive, attribute-based information. The information in a directory is generally read much more often than it is written. Uniface applications can connect to LDAP servers using the Uniface LDP connector. Using the LDAP V3 protocol, it can connect to LDAP servers, including stand-alone LDAP servers and X.500 Directory System Agents with an LDAP gateway installed. Related Links LDAP Data Structure LDAP data is stored in a record-like structure, called an entry. No table or field definition information is stored in the LDAP environment. Instead, each entry contains its own field definitions. Data Storage and Retrieval on LDAP LDAP differs substantially in structure from traditional relational databases. Map LDAP Entries to Uniface Entities You cannot use the database import facility with LDAP, so you must manually define a model that maps LDAP entries to Uniface entities. Configure Uniface for LDAP To configure Uniface to connect to with LDAP, you need to edit the Uniface application assignment file. LDAP Connector The Uniface LDAP connector enables you to connect with LDAP servers, including stand-alone LDAP servers and X.500 Directory System Agents with an LDAP gateway installed.
